item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: Fans were expecting big things from the 'Detroit Bridgerton Ball' but were left disappointed. Here's what happened
item.description: The Netflix-inspired event is drawing comparisons to the infamous Willy Wonka experience fiasco earlier this year.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
item.description: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Energy giant ENGIE admits misleading customer about power prices after 'deceptive and misleading' behaviour
item.description: The South Australian government informed the ACCC after global energy giant ENGIE admitted to incorrectly telling a customer that power prices are set by the government.

item.title: Traditional custodian readies for 'challenge' over North West Shelf gas project extension
item.description: Environment Minister Murray Watt approved the extension of Australia's biggest gas project last week.

item.title: Losing Streak
item.description: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

item.title: Socceroos far from a World Cup 'group of death' against United States, Paraguay and a European qualifier
item.description: Nothing can be guaranteed in a World Cup, but the Socceroos' draw alongside the United States, Paraguay and a European qualifier gives a solid shot at advancing, with the Australians avoiding most of the big names.

item.title: Socceroos FIFA World Cup 2026 times, dates and locations set, with games in Vancouver, Seattle and Santa Clara
item.description: Australia's North American west coast adventure at the World Cup is confirmed, with matches in Vancouver, Seattle and Santa Clara in California.

item.title: Solar to the fore as grid sails through heatwave and record demand
item.description: For so long, the pounding sunshine of an Australian heatwave was the grid's biggest threat. It has now become its greatest asset.

item.title: Socceroos midfielder Jackson Irvine ruled out 'until further notice' with recurrence of foot injury
item.description: Socceroos midfielder Jackson Irvine is facing an extended stint on the sidelines heading into this year's World Cup.

item.title: X to change AI chatbot after outrage over sexual deepfake images
item.description: New laws in the UK that would outlaw the generation of sexual deepfakes are set to come into force this week.

item.title: From Xinjiang to Tehran: Iran's unrest is shaping China's view of power and world order
item.description: Xi Jinping's ambition is to occupy a central position in a reshaped international system, not to lead a permanent grouping of sanctioned or crisis-prone states.

item.title: WA premier Roger Cook extends embattled Griffin Coal's lifeline, citing energy security concerns
item.description: Since 2022, the WA government has already tipped $300 million into propping up the insolvent Indian-owned miner, which is more than $1 billion in debt.

item.title: Paris Hilton brings sex tape story to Washington for AI deepfake porn fight
item.description: The party girl turned anti-abuse advocate throws her glamorous celebrity heft behind a push to tackle deepfake pornography.

item.title: Most AI assistants are feminine — and it's fuelling dangerous stereotypes and abuse
item.description: The design choices behind AI technologies — female voices, deferential responses, playful deflections — create a permissive environment for gendered aggression.

item.title: Cannington greyhound racing 'carnage' prompts phase out calls
item.description: Four dogs were injured and two euthanased after a greyhound racing event in Cannington this week, escalating calls to end the sport.

item.title: Inside the brand strategy behind the Beckham family drama — and who controls the narrative now
item.description: In a modern crisis, experts say control doesn't always come from speaking louder — but from knowing when, where and how briefly to speak at all.

item.title: Battery demand 'straps on a rocket' as rooftop solar passes its peak
item.description: Even as Australia appears to have passed the peak of solar uptake, demand for batteries is seemingly headed into orbit.

item.title: Diamonds defeat Jamaica in first netball Test match in Adelaide
item.description: The Diamonds made a flying start with the initial six goals in a comprehensive 72-41 victory at the Adelaide Entertainment Centre.

item.title: Big swings in Australia's electricity market have a frustrating effect on our power bills
item.description: The benefits of extremely low energy prices during the day are "washed away" by extremely high ones after dark. But there is light at the end of the tunnel.

item.title: Why gen Zs on social media are having 'a very Chinese time in my life'
item.description: Some Chinese Australians have mixed feelings about the sudden global embrace of Chinese culture after the trend emerged late last year.

item.title: Danish people using apps to help boycott American goods over Greenland
item.description: After Donald Trump's repeated threats to acquire Greenland, many Danish people felt powerless but new apps are helping them make choices to boycott US-made products.

item.title: Maritime safety regulator investigating Carnival cruise ship after whistleblower concerns
item.description: The maritime union says crew members of a major Carnival cruise ship allege they are being made to sleep in crowded rooms, lack access to safe free drinking water, and that some are earning as little as $2.50 an hour.

item.title: Planned nurse strike rescheduled at Lyell McEwin Hospital amid pay dispute
item.description: The South Australian government says plans from Lyell McEwin Hospital nurses to strike tomorrow have been postponed, amid an unresolved industrial dispute over pay and conditions.

item.title: Volunteer firefighters wasting hours 'babysitting' pole-top fires waiting for Western Power crews
item.description: Volunteer firefighters in regional Western Australia say fatigued crews are frustrated over time spent monitoring pole-top fires before Western Power crews travel to the scene.

item.title: How young people are coping with the rising cost of living
item.description: Many people are struggling with the cost of living and increasingly, young Australians say they are not just worried about the situation now, but also what it means for the future.

item.title: Players flee court at UAE tennis event after security alert amid Middle East war
item.description: An ATP Challenger tournament in Fujairah is halted abruptly due to a security alert, forcing players and officials to run from the courts to designated safe areas.

item.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz: How well doest thou know his characters and quotes? Testeth thineself hither
item.description: A quiz by any other word would smell as sweet, or something, so have a crack at our 10 questions on the works of William Shakespeare.

item.title: Two new novels, The Heir Apparent and Royal Spin, offer a glimpse behind the curtain of royal life
item.description: With Queen Mary of Denmark soon to arrive in Australia and scandal engulfing the House of Windsor, royals are swamping our news cycle. So why are readers so fascinated by royal fiction?

item.title: Indian Wells 2026: Talia Gibson scores upset win over Jasmine Paolini, Aryna Sabalenka outduels Naomi Osaka
item.description: Talia Gibson scores one of the biggest wins of her young career as she advances at Indian Wells, while Aryna Sabalenka shows off one of the newest members of her team.

item.title: Australia's pesticide regulator sets new rules banning some rat baits from public sale
item.description: Rat baits could soon be restricted to licensed operators after a change in regulations from the Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority.

item.title: The real-life story behind The Voice of Hind Rajab
item.description: Protests erupted following the killing of six-year-old Hind Rajab at the hands of Israeli forces. Two years later, her story is in the running for an Academy Award.

item.title: Tahmoor Colliery liquidators confirm up to 238 redundancies
item.description: Up to 238 workers at Tahmoor Colliery in south-west Sydney are being made redundant as liquidators attempt to sell the coal mine, following months of financial turmoil under Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance.

item.title: Former NRL player Chris Walker charged with alleged domestic violence offences
item.description: Former Queensland State of Origin player Chris Walker is due to face a Gold Coast court tomorrow after his arrest last night.

item.title: Fuel rationing a chance in Australia if war continues to trim global oil supplies, experts say
item.description: Experts say Australians should prepare for the possibility of widespread fuel rationing if the war in the Middle East is prolonged.

item.title: Tuesday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Rare Rymer apples found on NSW Southern Tablelands farm
item.description: The Rymer variety of cooking apple, once believed to have died out, has been found on a rural property in the NSW Southern Tablelands.

item.title: How to choose sheets and bed linen that last as long as possible
item.description: Shopping for sheets can be overwhelming and expensive. This is what textile experts say to look out for.

item.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
item.description: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

item.title: It's February 2020 all over again, but this time one man can end it
item.description: Though few Australians recognise it, our economic institutions have an exceptional track record of making good decisions in a crisis, but the uncertainty of Trump's war is a new type of challenge.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Show us your trivia smarts. Let's play, HARD!
item.description: Don't blame your poor performance on a slow-start Sunday. We all know the result would be the same on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday …

item.title: Markets are complacent as Iran war threatens global economy
item.description: The markets' benign assessment of the Iran war is wrong and the damage to the global economy could get worse.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 23
item.description: Can you remember who Will Smith slapped, or what happened during "sandpapergate" scandal? Test yourself with today's quiz.

item.title: International Energy Agency says second round of emergency fuel releases is on table
item.description: World leaders have failed to grasp the depth of the energy crisis created by the Iran war, the head of the International Energy Agency has warned, as he considers releasing extra emergency oil stores.

item.title: “The world is facing the greatest global energy security threat in history”
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Donald Trump emboldens TACO taunts over sudden Iranian strikes backdown
item.description: After threatening to "obliterate" Iran's power plants, Donald Trump's sudden backdown is again fuelling TACO taunts.

item.title: Australia's fuel security is exposed by Iran war but renewable energy offers an out
item.description: From UN leaders to former Australian Defence Force chiefs, a growing chorus of voices argues that dependence on fossil fuels is not just a climate issue, but a national security risk — exposing countries to volatile prices and supply shocks.

item.title: Proposed Aboriginal land rights law change limits unused Crown land access, councils say
item.description: Aboriginal councils who have fought for decades to secure ancestral land are concerned proposed law reform puts rights at risk.

item.title: Former defence leaders outline already-present fossil fuel dependence, climate disinformation threats
item.description: Australians are already deeply emersed in a global disinformation war about fossil fuels and climate change, former defence leaders say in an extensive new review.

item.title: The Kates, Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney, take crime comedy Deadloch to Darwin
item.description: Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney (Get Krack!n) set out to make a "funny Broadchurch" with crime comedy Deadloch. Now, with fans around the world, it's back for season two.

item.title: 25yo man dies in NT police custody after arrest and detainment in police car cage in Darwin
item.description: A 25-year-old Indigenous man has died in police custody in Darwin's northern suburbs overnight, with police saying he was "arrested without incident".

item.title: OpenAI pulls plug on AI video app Sora that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: Sora was released in September by the company behind ChatGPT and soon went viral as a place to share short-form videos generated by artificial intelligence, but it also raised alarms.

item.title: Meta and Google found liable in landmark social media addiction lawsuit
item.description: The case was brought on by a 20-year-old woman who accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms which worsened her mental health.

item.title: At 104, George Franklin is months away from becoming the world's oldest conductor
item.description: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

item.title: Eight-fold increase in EPA odour complaints in Melbourne's west linked to small number of businesses
item.description: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

item.title: Review of Tasmania's climate laws finds net zero status could be 'temporary'
item.description: Tasmania loves to boast about its net zero emissions. But a new report has found that status could be "temporary" if further actions are not taken.

item.title: Vanished: Six months of searching for Gus Lamont
item.description: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

item.title: More than 1,000 Australians physically assaulted every day, new ABS crime data shows
item.description: Almost 400,000 Australians experienced physical assault in 2024-2025, according to new victimisation data released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: Socceroos snatch late win against Cameroon as Jordy Bos delivers and teenager Lucas Herrington impresses
item.description: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

item.title: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia sign 'mutually beneficial' air defence deal
item.description: The deal comes at a crucial time for Kyiv, as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said weapons for Ukraine could be diverted to the Iran war

item.title: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The strange death of Liberal England and the lessons for Liberal Adelaide
item.description: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

item.title: End of an era for Tharwa General Store after a century being owned by one family
item.description: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

item.title: Two teenagers in custody, two on the run and paramedic injured after Melbourne crash
item.description: Police say four teenagers in a stolen car collided with an older couple in Carlton this morning, with two fleeing the scene immediately.

item.title: The Offbeat Sari challenges cliches made about the 'traditional' and 'uncomfortable' sari
item.description: For many South Asians, museums in the West have historically been places where their cultures were displayed as static artefacts. An exhibition like The Offbeat Sari has flipped that script, presenting South Asian creativity as innovative and globally influential.

item.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, ships make a bargain to negotiate their safe passage
item.description: Iran is running one of the world's most critical shipping lanes as a "toll booth" and has likely made hundreds of millions of dollars of extra income from selling its own oil since the US and Israel waged war against the regime.

item.title: Here's what a month of fuel hikes looks like across Australia
item.description: From crop farmers to wildlife rescuers, here are some of the stories ABC readers told us about how the fuel crisis is impacting them.

item.title: Iran warns US against ground invasion as key diplomats meet in Pakistan for talks
item.description: Iran's parliament speaker says Iran is prepared to confront any American forces on its soil and would set troops "on fire" in its warning against a US ground invasion.

item.title: Primary producers sell assets, consider lay-offs as fuel costs hit hard
item.description: The sudden rise in fuel costs is forcing farmers to make difficult decisions, such as laying off workers and selling equipment to keep their businesses running.

item.title: Regional WA businesses say it's a matter of weeks before fuel prices start hitting consumers
item.description: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

item.title: Scientists 'crack the code' as endangered palm cockatoo chick hatches in artificial nest
item.description: Scientists rejoice as adult birds enter an artificial hollow to feed and incubate the egg, before hatching and eventually flying away.

item.title: 'No Kings' protests against Donald Trump draw crowds across US and Europe
item.description: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

item.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
item.description: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

item.title: How Australia became hostage to fuel imports
item.description: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

item.title: Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub closing due to lack of funding, leaving First Nations patients in Canberra in the lurch
item.description: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

item.title: Trump threatens to seize Kharg Island, warns Iran to open Strait of Hormuz — as it happened
item.description: The US president wrote on a social media post that "great progress is being made" in talks with Iran but said that if a deal is not reached, the US would broaden its offensive.

item.title: Dezi Freeman killed by police in Victoria's north-east after seven-month hunt
item.description: Freeman had been wanted since last August over the fatal shooting of two police officers at Porepunkah.

item.title: Antarctic penguin appears on remote WA beach, 4,000km from home
item.description: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

item.title: Contemporary regional life looms large in major Brad Rimmer exhibition
item.description: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.

item.title: Government halves fuel excise for three months to ease cost of petrol, diesel
item.description: The government has halved the fuel excise on petrol and diesel for three months, reducing the cost of fuel by 26.3 cents per litre.

item.title: Israel Defense Forces admit to posting fake image of Lebanese journalist killed in strike
item.description: The IDF has admitted the image of a killed journalist in military uniform it posted on its official X account was faked.

item.title: Rare durian variety sells for $200 at auction during fruit festival in Innisfail
item.description: A rare fruit has snared top dollar at auction at a Far North Queensland festival over the weekend.

item.title: Key people and moments in seven-month search for police killer Dezi Freeman
item.description: Dezi Freeman fatally shot two officers and injured a third before he vanished into remote bushland. Here is what we know about the events leading up to the fatal shooting — and the months-long manhunt that followed.

item.title: Red sky seen in WA ahead of Cyclone Narelle makes global headlines
item.description: Cyclone Narelle turned the sky over parts of Western Australia's north into an ominous red, sparking headlines across the globe.

item.title: Dezi Freeman's death gives 'closure' to slain Victorian police officer Neal Thompson's friend
item.description: The best friend of slain Porepunkah detective Neal Thompson says the police shooting of fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman gives "closure", though it will not bring back his mate.

item.title: Drivers winched to safety after ignoring road closures in WA's flooded Gascoyne following Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

item.title: Alan Kohler Finance Report tumbnail
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: From tax cuts to price gouging crackdowns, how G7 nations are combating the fuel crisis
item.description: Australia is not alone in weathering rising fuel costs and issues with domestic supply and distribution amid the war in the Middle East. G7 nations are also working out ways to ease the crisis.

item.title: Some travel times in south-east Queensland could be twice as long in April. Here's what you need to know
item.description: A series of major track closures are set to take place across south-east Queensland from Easter, impacting travellers just as fuel prices begin to bite. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Sharon Fulton's killer Raymond Reddington sentenced to life in prison
item.description: Raymond Reddington is sentenced to life in prison with a minimum of 20 years for murdering Sharon Fulton in Perth in 1986, as her family continues to search for "her final resting place".

item.title: Five social media platforms investigated over compliance with under-16 ban
item.description: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

item.title: Investigations continue after police shoot fugitive Dezi Freeman dead
item.description: Police say they will come down hard on anyone who helped Dezi Freeman, who was shot dead by police yesterday after a massive seven-month manhunt.

item.title: Bittersweet Iraqi film The President's Cake brings hope as war rages in the Middle East
item.description: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

item.title: Insiders profit as Iran war pressure points build on global economy
item.description: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

item.title: Early government modelling assumes fuel rationing when stockpile falls to 10 days' diesel supply
item.description: Diesel stocks would last about six months if Australia's imports dropped by 20 per cent, according to modelling compiled for the federal government early in the Middle East war.

item.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
item.description: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: Donald Trump renews threats against Iran's energy infrastructure while his secretary of war faces an apparent rebuke from the pope.

item.title: The oil shock has completely reshaped Australia's petrol price cycle
item.description: Petrol stations in our biggest cities hiked prices ahead of global oil prices, but experts say their profit margins are now much slimmer.

item.title: The 'miracle' drugs giving children with spinal muscular atrophy another shot at life
item.description: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

item.title: Live: Israel intends to apply Gaza 'model' in Lebanon
item.description: Israel is planning to occupy Lebanon's south and repeat what it did in Gaza, according to Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz. Follow live.

item.title: Chicken and eggs could end up cheaper because of new high-protein sorghum variety
item.description: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

item.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adult employees
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

item.title: State government takes over decision-making on controversial Gold Coast quarry proposal
item.description: A decision on whether to proceed with a planned quarry at Reedy Creek on the Gold Coast will rest with the state government, after community opposition.

item.title: NASA begins countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years
item.description: Four astronauts on the Artemis II rocket are set to blast off early Thursday morning.

item.title: Tim Rogers on making a living, fatherhood and a You Am I classic
item.description: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

item.title: Is this image of a child climbing on a missile real? ABC NEWS Verify explains
item.description: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

item.title: Telstra defends coverage after new rules for mobile coverage maps introduced
item.description: ACMA has introduced new rules for standardised mobile coverage maps that will see mobile signals below -115 dBm labelled as "no coverage".

item.title: NSW premier calls for more sovereignty on national energy supply as petrol prices rise
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns calls for greater fuel security while unveiling the state's fuel savings measures, arguing for more electric vehicles and charging stations.

item.title: Tributes for farming stalwart Ian Burnett and grandson after farming accident
item.description: Tributes are flowing in for a central Queensland producer who died with his grandson when machinery rolled on a property at the weekend.

item.title: 'Welcome to Country' pioneer farewelled on her beloved Bundjalung Country
item.description: Rhoda Roberts AO is being remembered for her ability to laugh and embrace life, as well as her contribution to the Australian arts and Indigenous cultural movements.

item.title: Why these women have never had a partner
item.description: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

item.title: Zac Lomax available to make Super Rugby Pacific debut if selected for Western Force
item.description: Zac Lomax could get his first taste of rugby union at a venue where the Western Force recruit is no stranger to receiving a hostile welcome.

item.title: WA man Samuel Jingle sentenced to life in prison after killing woman in state's north
item.description: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

item.title: Devastated artists rally after Daly River floods destroy Merrepen Arts Centre
item.description: Artists from the remote Northern Territory community of Nauiyu are facing a devastating loss after the Merrepen Arts Centre was destroyed by recent flooding.

item.title: City of Melbourne plans to increase on-street parking fees, fines and rates, draft budget shows
item.description: The City of Melbourne's 2026-27 draft budget proposes an increase to on-street parking fees despite Lord Mayor Nick Reece's election promise to not raise fees.

item.title: FR Dan 1-30-2026
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Israel's ambassador defends new death penalty for Palestinians
item.description: Hillel Newman uses an address to the National Press Club to defend Israel's war with Iran and its military action in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Barbie fans paid hundreds of dollars for a festival in Florida. Here's why they weren't impressed
item.description: Barbie fans who paid up to $656 for an "unforgettable experience" were met with a concrete-floored warehouse and flimsy pink cardboard cut-outs.

item.title: Dezi Freeman's nephew Luke Filby speaks out about his 'evil' uncle's death at hands of Victoria police
item.description: When Dezi Freeman was shot dead it left his family with mixed emotions. Some are shattered, others know an "evil man" is dead. His nephew speaks out.
